 {"id":"beads_rust-3stn","title":"Integrate rich output into ready command","description":"## Command: br ready

### Traffic Level: HIGH  
The primary command for finding actionable work. Agents use this constantly to find what to work on next.

### Current Implementation
Location: src/cli/commands/ready.rs
Output: Table of issues with no blocking dependencies

### Integration Steps
1. Use IssueTable with highlight_ready(true)
2. Add 'ready' visual indicator (✓ or green highlight)
3. Show blocking count in additional column
4. Consider adding priority sorting emphasis

### Visual Enhancement
Ready issues should STAND OUT:
- Green left border or background tint
- Bold ID and title
- Checkmark icon prefix

### Table Columns
- ID (bold, green-tinted)
- Priority (heat-mapped)
- Title
- Type
- Labels
- Age (how long has it been ready?)

### Agent Optimization
The --robot flag must produce stable, parseable output:
- IDs one per line, or
- JSON array of IDs
No visual formatting that could break parsing.

### Sorting
Maintain current sort: priority desc, then created asc
Visual priority distinction helps humans scan quickly.

---

## Testing Requirements

### Unit Tests

```rust
fn test_ready_json_output_unchanged() {

    let current = run_ready_json();
    let baseline = load_fixture("tests/fixtures/json_baseline/ready.json");
    assert_json_eq!(current, baseline);
}

#[test]
fn test_ready_respects_robot_flag() {
    // --robot should produce parseable output without ANSI
    let output = run_ready_robot();
    assert!(!contains_ansi_codes(&output));
}

#[test]
fn test_ready_highlights_actionable_issues() {
    // In rich mode, ready issues should have green styling
    let ctx = OutputContext::rich();
    let table = IssueTable::new(&issues, &ctx).highlight_ready(true);
    // Verify styling applied
}
```

### E2E Test Script
Location: tests/e2e/ready_e2e.sh

```bash
#!/bin/bash
set -euo pipefail
source "$(dirname "$0")/../common/test_helpers.sh"

log_info "=== E2E: Ready Command ==="

# Setup
setup_test_db
create_test_issues_with_deps

# Test 1: Basic ready command
log_step "Testing basic ready command"
OUTPUT=$(br ready 2>&1)
assert_contains "$OUTPUT" "beads_rust-" "Should show issue IDs"
log_pass "Basic ready works"

# Test 2: JSON output unchanged
log_step "Testing JSON backward compatibility"
CURRENT=$(br ready --json | jq -S .)
BASELINE=$(cat tests/fixtures/json_baseline/ready.json | jq -S .)
assert_json_eq "$CURRENT" "$BASELINE" "JSON output should match baseline"
log_pass "JSON backward compatibility verified"

# Test 3: Robot mode
log_step "Testing robot mode"
ROBOT_OUTPUT=$(br ready --robot 2>&1)
assert_no_ansi "$ROBOT_OUTPUT" "Robot mode should have no ANSI codes"
log_pass "Robot mode clean"

# Test 4: Rich mode with TTY simulation
log_step "Testing rich mode (simulated TTY)"
RICH_OUTPUT=$(script -q /dev/null br ready 2>&1 || true)
# Verify we got some output (rich mode)
assert_not_empty "$RICH_OUTPUT" "Rich mode should produce output"
log_pass "Rich mode works"

log_success "=== Ready command E2E: ALL PASSED ==="
```

-{"id":"beads_rust-3uxa","title":"Self-update command: br update with signature verification","description":"# Self-Update Command Implementation

## Scope
Implement `br update` command for in-place binary updates with cryptographic verification.

## Deliverables
- `br update` - Check for and install updates
- `br update --check` - Check only, don't install
- `br update --force` - Force reinstall current version
- `br update --version <VERSION>` - Install specific version
- Ed25519 signature verification of downloaded binaries
- SHA256 hash verification as fallback
- Streaming downloads for large files with progress
- Atomic update (download to temp, verify, replace)
- Rollback on failure (preserve original binary)
- Version comparison (semver-aware)

## Technical Approach
- Use self_update crate (rustls backend, no OpenSSL)
- GitHub Releases as update source
- .sig files for Ed25519 signatures
- .sha256 files for hash verification

## Acceptance Criteria
- `br update` downloads and installs newer version in-place
- `br update --check` exits 0 if up-to-date, 1 if update available
- Update fails safely if signature invalid (original binary preserved)
- Update works even if current binary is corrupted
- Progress shown during download
- Works on all platforms (Linux, macOS, Windows)

## Unit Tests (src/cli/commands/update.rs)
- test_version_comparison: verify semver comparison logic
- test_signature_verification: verify Ed25519 signature validation
- test_hash_verification: verify SHA256 hash checking
- test_release_parsing: verify GitHub Releases API response parsing
- test_rollback_on_failure: verify original binary restored on error
- test_platform_asset_selection: verify correct binary selected per platform

## E2E Tests (tests/e2e_update.rs)
- e2e_update_check_uptodate: when on latest, exit 0 and print "up to date"
- e2e_update_check_available: when older, exit 1 and print available version
- e2e_update_to_latest: verify successful update replaces binary
- e2e_update_force: verify --force reinstalls current version
- e2e_update_bad_signature: verify update aborted, original preserved
- e2e_update_network_failure: verify graceful error handling
- e2e_update_specific_version: verify --version flag works

## Logging Requirements
- [UPDATE] Checking for updates...
- [UPDATE] Current: v0.9.0, Latest: v1.0.0
- [DOWNLOAD] Downloading br-v1.0.0-linux-x86_64.tar.gz (27.4MB)
- [VERIFY] Signature: VALID (Ed25519)
- [VERIFY] SHA256: OK
- [UPDATE] Replacing binary...
- [SUCCESS] Updated to v1.0.0","status":"in_progress","priority":1,"issue_type":"task","assignee":"BronzeHawk","created_at":"2026-01-21T00:49:46.653237917Z","created_by":"ubuntu","updated_at":"2026-01-21T04:34:56.201423581Z","compaction_level":0,"original_size":0,"dependencies":[{"issue_id":"beads_rust-3uxa","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-1hof","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T00:52:24.414893818Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-3uxa","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-36jt","type":"blocks","created_at":"2026-01-21T00:52:08.687326174Z","created_by":"ubuntu"},{"issue_id":"beads_rust-3uxa","depends_on_id":"beads_rust-7nh","type":"parent-child","created_at":"2026-01-21T00:49:46.701284113Z","created_by":"ubuntu"}],"comments":[{"id":152,"issue_id":"beads_rust-3uxa","author":"Dicklesworthstone","text":"Enabled signature verification in 'br upgrade' by integrating 'self_update' with 'signatures' feature and embedding 'release_public_key.pem'. Generated Ed25519 keypair and committed public key. Note: private key was not committed.","created_at":"2026-01-21T00:57:40Z"},{"id":159,"issue_id":"beads_rust-3uxa","author":"CopperMountain","text":"## Signature Verification Status Analysis

**Current State:** Signature verification code is commented out in upgrade.rs (lines 242-249, 260-269).

**Critical Finding:** Format mismatch between release pipeline and self_update crate:
- Release workflow uses minisign (.minisig format)
- self_update crate expects raw Ed25519 signatures (.sig files)

These formats are incompatible. The release_public_key.bin (32 bytes raw Ed25519) cannot verify minisign signatures.

**Options:**
1. Change release workflow to generate raw Ed25519 .sig files
2. Use only SHA256 checksum verification (already working)
3. Add minisign verification as separate post-download step

**Recommendation:** Keep signature verification disabled until format is aligned. Current upgrade implementation (--check, --force, --version, --dry-run, JSON/rich output) is complete and functional.","created_at":"2026-01-21T03:05:08Z"}]}

 {"id":"beads_rust-4n9","title":"Unit Test Infrastructure","description":"# Unit Test Infrastructure

## Purpose
Establish comprehensive unit testing infrastructure with fixtures, helpers, and test utilities. This bead ensures every module has thorough unit test coverage with detailed logging for debugging test failures.

## Files to Create

### tests/common/mod.rs
```rust
//! Shared test utilities and fixtures for all unit tests.

use beads_rust::storage::SqliteStorage;
use beads_rust::model::{Issue, IssueType, Status, Priority, Dependency, Comment};
use tempfile::TempDir;
use std::sync::Once;

static INIT: Once = Once::new();

/// Initialize test logging (call once per test module)
pub fn init_test_logging() {
    INIT.call_once(|| {
        tracing_subscriber::fmt()
            .with_env_filter("beads_rust=debug,test=debug")
            .with_test_writer()
            .try_init()
            .ok();
    });
}

/// Create an in-memory database for unit tests
pub fn test_db() -> SqliteStorage {
    init_test_logging();
    SqliteStorage::open_memory().expect("Failed to create test database")
}

/// Create a file-backed database in a temp directory
pub fn test_db_with_dir() -> (SqliteStorage, TempDir) {
    init_test_logging();
    let dir = TempDir::new().expect("Failed to create temp dir");
    let db_path = dir.path().join(".beads").join("beads.db");
    std::fs::create_dir_all(db_path.parent().unwrap()).unwrap();
    let storage = SqliteStorage::open(&db_path).expect("Failed to create test database");
    (storage, dir)
}
```

### tests/common/fixtures.rs
```rust
//! Test data generators and fixtures.

use beads_rust::model::*;
use chrono::Utc;

/// Create a basic test issue with sensible defaults
pub fn issue(title: &str) -> Issue {
    Issue {
        id: format!("test-{}", hash_title(title)),
        title: title.to_string(),
        description: None,
        issue_type: IssueType::Task,
        status: Status::Open,
        priority: Priority::Medium,
        assignee: None,
        labels: vec![],
        parent_id: None,
        created_at: Utc::now(),
        updated_at: Utc::now(),
        closed_at: None,
        defer_until: None,
        due_date: None,
        content_hash: None,
    }
}

/// Builder pattern for complex test issues
pub struct IssueBuilder {
    issue: Issue,
}

impl IssueBuilder {
    pub fn new(title: &str) -> Self {
        Self { issue: issue(title) }
    }

    pub fn with_type(mut self, t: IssueType) -> Self {
        self.issue.issue_type = t;
        self
    }

    pub fn with_status(mut self, s: Status) -> Self {
        self.issue.status = s;
        self
    }

    pub fn with_priority(mut self, p: Priority) -> Self {
        self.issue.priority = p;
        self
    }

    pub fn with_assignee(mut self, a: &str) -> Self {
        self.issue.assignee = Some(a.to_string());
        self
    }

    pub fn with_labels(mut self, labels: &[&str]) -> Self {
        self.issue.labels = labels.iter().map(|s| s.to_string()).collect();
        self
    }

    pub fn with_description(mut self, desc: &str) -> Self {
        self.issue.description = Some(desc.to_string());
        self
    }

    pub fn build(self) -> Issue {
        self.issue
    }
}

/// Create N issues with sequential titles
pub fn issues(count: usize, prefix: &str) -> Vec<Issue> {
    (0..count)
        .map(|i| issue(&format!("{} {}", prefix, i + 1)))
        .collect()
}

/// Create a dependency between two issues
pub fn dependency(from: &str, to: &str) -> Dependency {
    Dependency {
        issue_id: from.to_string(),
        depends_on_id: to.to_string(),
        dep_type: DepType::Blocks,
        created_at: Utc::now(),
    }
}

/// Create a chain of dependencies: A -> B -> C -> ...
pub fn dependency_chain(ids: &[&str]) -> Vec<Dependency> {
    ids.windows(2)
        .map(|pair| dependency(pair[0], pair[1]))
        .collect()
}

/// Create a comment on an issue
pub fn comment(issue_id: &str, body: &str, author: &str) -> Comment {
    Comment {
        id: format!("cmt-{}", Utc::now().timestamp_nanos()),
        issue_id: issue_id.to_string(),
        body: body.to_string(),
        author: author.to_string(),
        created_at: Utc::now(),
    }
}
```

### tests/common/assertions.rs
```rust
//! Custom assertions with detailed logging on failure.

use beads_rust::model::Issue;
use tracing::{info, error};

/// Assert issue exists in storage with detailed logging
#[track_caller]
pub fn assert_issue_exists(storage: &impl Storage, id: &str) {
    info!("Asserting issue exists: {}", id);
    match storage.get_issue(id) {
        Ok(Some(issue)) => {
            info!("Found issue: {:?}", issue);
        }
        Ok(None) => {
            error!("Issue not found: {}", id);
            panic!("Expected issue '{}' to exist, but it was not found", id);
        }
        Err(e) => {
            error!("Error fetching issue {}: {:?}", id, e);
            panic!("Error fetching issue '{}': {:?}", id, e);
        }
    }
}

/// Assert issue has expected status
#[track_caller]
pub fn assert_status(storage: &impl Storage, id: &str, expected: Status) {
    let issue = storage.get_issue(id).unwrap().unwrap();
    if issue.status != expected {
        error!(
            "Status mismatch for {}: expected {:?}, got {:?}",
            id, expected, issue.status
        );
        panic!(
            "Expected issue '{}' to have status {:?}, but got {:?}",
            id, expected, issue.status
        );
    }
    info!("Status verified: {} is {:?}", id, expected);
}

/// Assert issue is blocked by specific issues
#[track_caller]
pub fn assert_blocked_by(storage: &impl Storage, id: &str, blockers: &[&str]) {
    let blocked = storage.get_blocked_issues().unwrap();
    let issue_blocked = blocked.iter().find(|b| b.issue.id == id);

    match issue_blocked {
        Some(b) => {
            for blocker in blockers {
                if !b.blocker_ids.contains(&blocker.to_string()) {
                    error!(
                        "Issue {} not blocked by {}, actual blockers: {:?}",
                        id, blocker, b.blocker_ids
                    );
                    panic!("Expected {} to be blocked by {}", id, blocker);
                }
            }
            info!("Verified {} is blocked by {:?}", id, blockers);
        }
        None => {
            error!("Issue {} is not blocked at all", id);
            panic!("Expected {} to be blocked", id);
        }
    }
}

/// Assert issue is ready (not blocked)
#[track_caller]
pub fn assert_ready(storage: &impl Storage, id: &str) {
    let ready = storage.get_ready_issues(100).unwrap();
    if !ready.iter().any(|i| i.id == id) {
        let blocked = storage.get_blocked_issues().unwrap();
        if let Some(b) = blocked.iter().find(|b| b.issue.id == id) {
            error!(
                "Issue {} is blocked by {:?}, not ready",
                id, b.blocker_ids
            );
        }
        panic!("Expected {} to be ready", id);
    }
    info!("Verified {} is ready", id);
}
```

### tests/common/scenarios.rs
```rust
//! Pre-built test scenarios for common testing patterns.

use super::*;

/// Set up a database with N issues, no dependencies
pub fn scenario_simple_issues(count: usize) -> (SqliteStorage, Vec<Issue>) {
    let storage = test_db();
    let issues = issues(count, "Issue");
    for issue in &issues {
        storage.create_issue(issue).unwrap();
    }
    (storage, issues)
}

/// Set up a database with a linear dependency chain
/// Returns: storage, issues (in dependency order, first blocks second, etc.)
pub fn scenario_linear_deps(count: usize) -> (SqliteStorage, Vec<Issue>) {
    let storage = test_db();
    let issues = issues(count, "Chain");
    for issue in &issues {
        storage.create_issue(issue).unwrap();
    }

    // Create chain: issues[0] blocks issues[1] blocks issues[2] ...
    for i in 1..issues.len() {
        let dep = dependency(&issues[i].id, &issues[i-1].id);
        storage.add_dependency(&dep).unwrap();
    }

    storage.rebuild_blocked_cache().unwrap();
    (storage, issues)
}

/// Set up a diamond dependency pattern
/// A depends on B and C, B and C both depend on D
pub fn scenario_diamond_deps() -> (SqliteStorage, Vec<Issue>) {
    let storage = test_db();
    let issues = vec![
        IssueBuilder::new("Top (A)").build(),
        IssueBuilder::new("Left (B)").build(),
        IssueBuilder::new("Right (C)").build(),
        IssueBuilder::new("Bottom (D)").build(),
    ];

    for issue in &issues {
        storage.create_issue(issue).unwrap();
    }

    // A depends on B and C
    storage.add_dependency(&dependency(&issues[0].id, &issues[1].id)).unwrap();
    storage.add_dependency(&dependency(&issues[0].id, &issues[2].id)).unwrap();
    // B and C depend on D
    storage.add_dependency(&dependency(&issues[1].id, &issues[3].id)).unwrap();
    storage.add_dependency(&dependency(&issues[2].id, &issues[3].id)).unwrap();

    storage.rebuild_blocked_cache().unwrap();
    (storage, issues)
}

/// Set up issues with various statuses
pub fn scenario_mixed_status() -> (SqliteStorage, Vec<Issue>) {
    let storage = test_db();
    let issues = vec![
        IssueBuilder::new("Open issue").with_status(Status::Open).build(),
        IssueBuilder::new("In progress").with_status(Status::InProgress).build(),
        IssueBuilder::new("Closed issue").with_status(Status::Closed).build(),
    ];

    for issue in &issues {
        storage.create_issue(issue).unwrap();
    }

    (storage, issues)
}
```

## Test Coverage Requirements

Every module MUST have unit tests covering:

1. **Happy path** - Normal operation
2. **Edge cases** - Empty inputs, boundary values
3. **Error cases** - Invalid inputs, expected failures
4. **Concurrent access** (where applicable)

### Minimum Test Cases Per Module

| Module | Required Test Count | Focus Areas |
|--------|---------------------|-------------|
| storage/sqlite.rs | 30+ | CRUD, transactions, pragmas |
| model/issue.rs | 15+ | Validation, serialization |
| model/types.rs | 10+ | Enum conversions, Display |
| error/mod.rs | 20+ | All error variants |
| cli/commands/*.rs | 10+ each | Args parsing, execution |
| sync/export.rs | 15+ | JSONL format, ordering |
| sync/import.rs | 15+ | Parsing, conflict handling |

## Logging in Tests

All tests MUST use structured logging:

```rust
#[test]
fn test_create_issue() {
    init_test_logging();
    info!("Starting test_create_issue");

    let storage = test_db();
    let issue = issue("Test issue");

    info!(?issue, "Creating issue");
    storage.create_issue(&issue).unwrap();

    info!(id = %issue.id, "Verifying issue was created");
    let retrieved = storage.get_issue(&issue.id).unwrap().unwrap();

    assert_eq!(retrieved.title, issue.title);
    info!("test_create_issue completed successfully");
}
```

Run tests with logging:
```bash
RUST_LOG=debug cargo test -- --nocapture
```

## Acceptance Criteria
- [ ] tests/common/mod.rs with test_db() and test_db_with_dir()
- [ ] tests/common/fixtures.rs with IssueBuilder and generators
- [ ] tests/common/assertions.rs with detailed logging assertions
- [ ] tests/common/scenarios.rs with pre-built test scenarios
- [ ] All test utilities use tracing for detailed output
- [ ] Documentation for adding new tests
- [ ] At least 150 unit tests across all modules
- [ ] Test coverage > 80% for core modules

## Dependencies
- Requires Model Types (test data)
- Requires Error Handling (error testing)
- Requires SQLite Storage Layer (storage tests)
- Phase 1 completion for full test infrastructure

## Rationale
Comprehensive unit tests catch bugs early and provide confidence during refactoring. The test infrastructure with fixtures and scenarios reduces boilerplate and ensures consistent test patterns. Detailed logging makes test failures easy to diagnose - when a test fails in CI, the logs tell you exactly what happened.
